sources.title: Navy Blues (1929)
sources.description: I've been researching newly public domain films from 1929 and earlier, so I'm uploading the best possible copies that I can find. Navy Blues is a rarely remembered romantic comedy starring William Haines and Anita Page. Reviews at the time were tepid and the box office was weak, but it still served as a launching point for William Haines between silent and talking films.

sources.title: Navy Blues (MGM, 1929) WILLIAM HAINES🍕 ANITA PAGE🍕 ROMANTIC COMEDY
sources.description:
PizzaFLIX Presents CLASSIC MOVIE MONDAY
Stars: William Haines, Anita Page, Karl Dane
Director: Clarence Brown
Set sail for laughter and romance with a rare early talkie from MGM starring lovebirds William Haines and Anita Page! Directed by acclaimed filmmaker Clarence Brown, this pre-Code romantic comedy blends sharp wit, sailor shenanigans, and old-Hollywood charm. Haines, known for his silent film success, shines in his first sound film, bringing charisma and comic timing as Jack Kelly, a navy bad-boy with a big heart. So successful was Haines' transition to sound that in 1930, one year after Navy Blues was released, he reigned as Hollywood's #1 male box-office star.

Anita Page, his co-star from Speedway, delivers a touching performance as the woman determined to tame this rogue sailor. Featuring Karl Dane and packed with 1920s nostalgia, Navy Blues showcases the Golden Age of Hollywood, with classic naval themes, heartwarming romance, and the signature William Haines’ charm.  A must-watch for fans of classic movies, silent-to-sound transitions, vintage naval comedies, and early MGM film history.

Are you a PizzaFLIX Fanatic? Join our exclusive Fan Club for dedicated Classic movie lovers: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCE34fwgW7kWr7tc7YiOWtRw/join

🍕 Stream now on PizzaFLIX—your home for rare and restored classic films. Don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe for more vintage gems starring legends like William Haines, Anita Page, and Clarence Brown.

#WilliamHaines #AnitaPage #ClarenceBrown #NavyBlues1929 #ClassicComedy #MGM #PizzaFLIX #OldHollywood #VintageRomance #SailorComedy

metadata.Plot: On shore leave, a young sailor meets and falls in love with a pretty young blonde. He goes home with her to meet her parents, but they don't approve of him at all. Their daughter takes offense at this, and in the ensuing argument she storms out of the house determined to live on her own. She believes that her new boyfriend will marry her, but when he admits that he's not the marrying kind and then goes back to sea, she feels hurt and rejected and her life goes into a downward spiral.
metadata.Writer: Raymond L. Schrock, Dale Van Every, J.C. Nugent
metadata.Actors: William Haines, Anita Page, Karl Dane
